title: Per-stage build telemetry
description: Wall-clock timings emitted to dist/build-telemetry.json by tools/build/build.py once per non-reproducible build. Captures the elapsed milliseconds each pipeline stage took, in the order the pipeline executed them. Adding a new stage is non-breaking; renaming or removing the schema_version field is a major bump.

$schema.description: Pointer to this schema so consumers can locate it without an out-of-band fetch.

schema_version.description: Semver of the build-telemetry.json wire format.

platform.description: Output of platform.platform(); coarse OS/arch identifier for cross-machine comparisons.

python.description: Major.Minor.Micro of the Python interpreter that ran the build.

stages.description: Number of stage records in the stages array.

duration_ms.description: Total wall-clock build duration in milliseconds.

stages.description: Per-stage timing in pipeline-execution order. Empty when the build runs zero stages (e.g. --only with an empty list).

stage.description: Pipeline stage name. Must be one of the canonical stages, but the schema does not enumerate them so a new stage can land without a same-PR schema bump.

duration_ms.description: Wall-clock duration of this stage in milliseconds.
